headline: A 2.86% Yield, 0.08% Fee, and 75 Stocks: Is HDV the Anti-Hype ETF?
summary: Dividend funds pulled in $24.1 billion in the first quarter of 2026, their strongest opening quarter in four years, and a lot of that cash went looking for a boring place to hide from AI-hype exhaustion. The iShares Core High Dividend ETF (NYSEARCA:HDV) is one of them. HDV holds 75 positions, charges 0.08%, and yields ... A 2.86% Yield, 0.08% Fee, and 75 Stocks: Is HDV the Anti-Hype ETF?

headline: AbbVie Announces TEPKINLY庐 (epcoritamab) in Combination with Lenalidomide and Rituximab is Approved by the European Commission for the Treatment of Relapsed or Refractory Follicular Lymphoma
summary: AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV) today announced that the European Commission (EC) granted marketing authorization for TEPKINLY庐 (epcoritamab) in combination with lenalidomide and rituximab (TEPKINLY + R2) for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed or refractory (R/R) follicular lymphoma (FL). The approval is based on results from the pivotal Phase 3 EPCORE庐 FL-1 trial, which evaluated fixed-duration TEPKINLY in combination with R2 compared to standard of care R2.

headline: Wall Street Got the Weak Jobs Report It Wanted. Chip Stocks Wrecked the Rally Anyway.
summary: Thursday's jobs report came in far below forecasts, initially easing pressure on the Federal Reserve, before a fresh slide in memory and semiconductor stocks dragged the Nasdaq lower even as the Dow closed at a record. Tesla sold off despite its best delivery quarter in years, healthcare names caught a bid, and SK Hynix lined up what would be the largest US listing ever by a foreign company.

raw.headline: Wall Street Got the Weak Jobs Report It Wanted. Chip Stocks Wrecked the Rally Anyway.
